articles.title: Tragic Fire Claims Life of Milnrow Mother
articles.excerpt: Laura Foulis, a mother from Milnrow, has died following a devastating fire at Higher Tunshill Farm. Emergency services responded to the incident on July 3.
articles.content_html:
A tragic incident occurred at Higher Tunshill Farm in Milnrow, where Laura Foulis lost her life in a fire that broke out in the stables.

Emergency services, including police and firefighters, were dispatched to the scene on July 3 to manage the situation and provide assistance.

The community is mourning the loss of Foulis, who has been described as a 'beautiful' mother, highlighting the impact of this tragedy on local residents.

Authorities are investigating the circumstances surrounding the fire, with further updates expected as the inquiry progresses.

articles.title: Investigation Underway Following Fatal Farm Fire in Rochdale
articles.excerpt: A woman in her 30s has tragically died in a fire at a farm in Rochdale, prompting a murder investigation and the arrest of a man.
articles.content_html:
A serious incident has unfolded in Rochdale where a woman in her 30s lost her life in a fire at a farm. The blaze has led to a murder investigation being launched by local authorities.

In connection with this tragic event, a man has been taken into custody. The circumstances surrounding the fire are currently under scrutiny as investigators work to establish the details of the incident.

Emergency services responded to the scene, but despite their efforts, the woman was pronounced dead. The investigation aims to uncover the cause of the fire and the events leading up to it.

Authorities are appealing for any witnesses or individuals with information related to the incident to come forward. This case has raised significant concern within the community, highlighting the need for safety and vigilance in residential areas.

articles.title: Transport Updates: Lift Out of Service at Central Park Tram Stop
articles.excerpt: Passengers using the Central Park tram stop towards Rochdale should be aware that the lift is currently out of service, affecting step-free access.
articles.content_html:
The Central Park tram stop is experiencing a disruption as the lift on the platform towards Rochdale via Oldham is out of service. This situation has been reported since the morning of 8 July and will remain until further notice.

For those travelling towards Oldham, alternative step-free access is available via a lift from The Gateway to the Manchester bound platform. Passengers will need to use the track crossing to reach the Oldham bound platform.

In addition to this, the lift at Freehold tram stop towards Manchester is also currently out of service. Passengers are advised to use the lift from Robinson Street to access the Oldham bound platform and then cross the tracks to reach the Manchester bound platform.

These service changes are part of ongoing improvements and maintenance across the network. Passengers are encouraged to plan their journeys accordingly and check for any further updates on the TfGM travel alerts page.

articles.title: Trial of Rat Run Closure in Heywood Sparks Mixed Reactions
articles.excerpt: The trial closure of a rat run in Heywood has elicited varied responses from local residents and commuters. While some support the initiative for reducing traffic, others express concerns about potential congestion.
articles.content_html:
A trial closure of a rat run in Heywood has commenced, leading to a range of reactions from the community. The initiative aims to address traffic issues in the area, but opinions on its effectiveness are divided.

Supporters of the closure believe it will enhance road safety and reduce the volume of traffic in residential areas. They argue that limiting access will create a more pleasant environment for local residents.

Conversely, some residents and commuters are worried about the possibility of increased congestion on alternative routes. They fear that redirecting traffic could lead to longer travel times and frustration for drivers.

The trial is part of ongoing efforts to improve traffic management in Heywood, with local authorities keen to assess the impact of such measures. Feedback from the community will be crucial in determining the future of the closure.

As the trial progresses, it remains to be seen how it will affect daily commutes and local traffic patterns. The mixed reactions highlight the challenges of balancing road safety with the needs of motorists.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article was compiled from identified public sources and may be updated when further verified information becomes available.
articles.right_to_reply: Anyone directly affected may request a correction or right of reply by emailing news@rochdaledaily.co.uk.

articles.title: Local Volunteer Honoured as Hidden Hero for Community Dedication
articles.excerpt: A Shaw resident has been celebrated for her significant contributions to Dunwood Park, receiving the Hidden Hero award for her volunteer work.
articles.content_html:
Janet Nuttall, a dedicated volunteer from Shaw, has been awarded the title of Hidden Hero for June by First Choice Homes Oldham. This recognition highlights her commitment to the Friends of Dunwood Park, where she has been actively involved for approximately a year.

At 68 years old, Nuttall, a retired HR administrator, plays a crucial role in the group, focusing on grants and fundraising efforts. Her background in bookkeeping and financial management has been instrumental in securing funding for various projects aimed at enhancing the park.

Nuttall was nominated for the award by Joseph Beeston, a parish councillor and chair of the Friends of Dunwood Park. He praised her unwavering dedication and the positive impact she has made within the community.

In appreciation of her efforts, Nuttall received a certificate along with a £50 shopping voucher presented by Rebecca Poff, the neighbourhood coordinator for Shaw and Crompton.

Expressing her gratitude, Nuttall shared her joy at being recognised, stating that her involvement in improving the park is driven by a desire to give back to her community, where she has lived for four decades. She emphasised that her motivation is not for accolades but for the love of the community.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article was compiled from identified public sources and may be updated when further verified information becomes available.
articles.right_to_reply: Anyone directly affected may request a correction or right of reply by emailing news@rochdaledaily.co.uk.

articles.title: Sunny Weather Forecast for Rochdale This Week
articles.excerpt: Rochdale is set to experience a warm and sunny week ahead, with temperatures reaching up to 31°C on Friday. The forecast indicates minimal chances of rain and high pollen levels, making it ideal for outdoor activities.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale is expected to enjoy a warm and sunny week, according to the latest weather forecast. Temperatures are predicted to peak at 31°C on Friday, July 10, with a minimum nighttime temperature of 17°C.

The weather will remain predominantly sunny throughout the week, with Saturday seeing a high of 28°C and Sunday dropping slightly to 26°C. Rain is forecasted to be minimal, with chances below 10% on most days.

Pollen levels are reported to be very high, particularly on Thursday and Friday, which may affect those with allergies. Residents are advised to take precautions if they are sensitive to pollen.

Wind speeds will vary, with gusts reaching up to 24mph on Saturday. Overall, the week promises pleasant weather, making it a great opportunity for outdoor activities and enjoying the summer.

articles.title: Plans for Temporary Fire Station in Heywood Advance
articles.excerpt: Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service is moving forward with plans for a temporary fire station in Heywood while the existing facility is rebuilt.
articles.content_html:
Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service (GMFRS) is set to construct a temporary fire station in Heywood as part of a redevelopment project for the existing site.

The new station will be located behind the current facility on Middleton Road, which is approximately 60 years old and deemed in need of replacement.

The temporary structure will be a two-storey modular building designed to accommodate one fire engine, while an existing outbuilding will house additional specialist vehicles.

This initiative is part of a broader £38 million Estates Strategy aimed at modernising fire stations across Greater Manchester to enhance service delivery.

Planning documents indicate that the temporary station will maintain emergency response capabilities during the redevelopment, ensuring uninterrupted service for the community.

A separate planning application for the demolition of the old station and the construction of a new community fire station, which will feature improved facilities, is expected to be submitted soon.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article was compiled from identified public sources and may be updated when further verified information becomes available.

articles.title: Pakistan Refuses to Accept Deportation of Rochdale Grooming Gang Leader
articles.excerpt: A diplomatic dispute has arisen as Pakistan declines to take back Shabir Ahmed, the ringleader of a Rochdale grooming gang, while the UK government seeks to amend laws for his deportation.
articles.content_html:
Pakistan has refused to accept the deportation of Shabir Ahmed, the leader of a grooming gang linked to Rochdale. This refusal has sparked a diplomatic row, with Pakistan demanding the extradition of two political dissidents in exchange for Ahmed's return.

The UK government is preparing to amend the Immigration Act of 1971 to facilitate Ahmed's deportation. The Home Secretary is expected to outline these changes soon, aiming to close existing legal loopholes that currently prevent his removal despite his Pakistani citizenship.

Ahmed's case has drawn significant attention, highlighting ongoing issues surrounding the deportation of foreign nationals convicted of serious crimes in the UK. The situation remains fluid as negotiations between the UK and Pakistan continue.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article is based on publicly available information and does not imply any guilt or legal status of individuals mentioned.

The Active Travel programme, delivered by Your Trust Rochdale in collaboration with Rochdale Borough Council and Transport for Greater Manchester, aims to improve public health, air quality, and reduce traffic congestion.

Statistics indicate that millions of journeys in Greater Manchester could be made on foot or by bike, highlighting the potential for significant change in travel habits.

The initiative includes various activities designed to support and facilitate walking and cycling throughout the borough, making these options more accessible and appealing to residents.

For those interested in learning more or participating in active travel activities, the Active Travel team can be contacted via email or phone for further information.

articles.title: Plans for New SEND School on Kingsway West Business Park
articles.excerpt: A proposal to convert a vacant warehouse into a school for children with special educational needs and disabilities has been submitted by Whitehall Academy.
articles.content_html:
A new educational facility catering to children with special educational needs and disabilities (SEND) is being proposed for a vacant warehouse located at Kingsway West Business Park in Rochdale.

The plans, submitted by Whitehall Academy, aim to transform the empty unit into a school accommodating up to 50 pupils. The design includes six classrooms, three sensory rooms, a multi-purpose hall, and various support spaces such as offices and a medical room.

The exterior of the building will remain unchanged, but a secure play area with artificial grass will be created on the west side, replacing previously unused space.

This new school would serve as an extension of Whitehall Academy's existing premises on Whitehall Street, which is a specialist independent primary school focused on supporting vulnerable children with complex needs.

Students at the proposed school may have diagnoses including autism and learning difficulties, and all will have an Education, Health, and Care Plan (EHCP) or be in the assessment process.

The school is expected to operate from 9am to 3pm for pupils, with staff hours extending from 8am to 4pm, potentially including breakfast and after-school clubs that could extend the day until 5pm.

The warehouse was previously occupied by Circuitmaster Designs Ltd, an engineering firm, and has been vacant since October 2024.

articles.title: Trial Underway for Man Accused of Murdering Teenage E-Bike Thief in Middleton
articles.excerpt: Terrence King is on trial for the alleged murder of 15-year-old Devon Simmonds-Caines, who died after being struck by King's car in July 2025.
articles.content_html:
Terrence King, 60, is currently on trial at Manchester's Minshull Street Crown Court, facing allegations of murdering 15-year-old Devon Simmonds-Caines. The incident occurred on 22 July 2025 in Middleton, Greater Manchester, when King allegedly drove his car at Simmonds-Caines in an act of revenge for the theft of an e-bike.

The prosecution, led by Michael Hayton KC, claims that King's actions were intentional and not a result of poor driving. CCTV footage presented in court reportedly shows Simmonds-Caines riding the stolen e-bike before King accelerated towards him, mounting the pavement and colliding with the boy.

Simmonds-Caines sustained fatal injuries and was pronounced dead at the scene. The footage was described as distressing, prompting some family members to leave the courtroom in tears.

While King has admitted to manslaughter, he denies the charge of murder, asserting that his actions were not premeditated. The trial continues as the jury examines the evidence surrounding the tragic incident.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This report is based on court proceedings and allegations. All individuals are presumed innocent until proven guilty in a court of law.
articles.right_to_reply: Anyone directly affected may request a correction or right of reply by emailing news@rochdaledaily.co.uk.

articles.title: Delays on Metrolink Services Affecting Rochdale

articles.content_html:
Passengers using the Metrolink services towards Rochdale Town Centre are currently experiencing delays. This disruption is attributed to a points fault at Trafford Bar, affecting multiple lines.

The lines impacted include those running from Altrincham to Bury, Manchester Airport to Victoria, and East Didsbury to Rochdale Town Centre. Services from Altrincham to Piccadilly and the Etihad Campus are also affected.

Metrolink has advised that some passengers may need to wait longer than usual for their services. They have issued an apology for any inconvenience caused during this time.

As of the latest update, the delays are expected to continue until further notice. Passengers are encouraged to check for live updates and plan their journeys accordingly.

articles.title: College Bank Relocation Programme Hits 100 Home Offers
articles.excerpt: Rochdale Boroughwide Housing has achieved a significant milestone in its relocation initiative, with over 100 residents now offered new homes as part of the College Bank estate transition.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale Boroughwide Housing (RBH) has announced that it has reached a key milestone in its College Bank relocation programme, with the 100th resident accepting an offer for a new home.

So far, 64 residents have successfully moved out of the high-rise blocks as the programme continues to assist those still living in the College Bank estate.

RBH is actively engaging with remaining residents to evaluate their housing needs, offering support through scheduled meetings and a twice-weekly drop-in session at the Town Mill Brow community room.

One resident, Tony, who recently relocated from Dunkirk Rise, shared his positive experience, highlighting the support he received from the RBH team, which he described as outstanding.

Tony noted that his move has significantly improved his living conditions and finances, allowing him to save on heating costs and even purchase a new car.

Hannah Fleming, RBH's Director of Regeneration and Asset Performance, expressed pride in the team's efforts and reaffirmed the organisation's commitment to ensuring the safety and well-being of all residents during this transition.

articles.excerpt: The Rochdale AFC First Team has resumed training at Platt Lane Sports Complex, marking the start of their pre-season preparations.
articles.content_html:
On Monday, the players of Rochdale AFC's First Team returned to the training pitch at Platt Lane Sports Complex. This marks the beginning of their pre-season preparations ahead of the upcoming football season.

The return to training is a significant moment for the team as they gear up for the challenges ahead. The players will be focusing on fitness and tactics as they aim to build a strong foundation for the season.

Platt Lane Sports Complex has been a key location for the club, providing facilities for training and development. The players are expected to engage in various drills and exercises to enhance their performance.

articles.title: Police Officer Hospitalised Following Middleton Crash Involving Police Van
articles.excerpt: A police officer has been taken to hospital after a crash involving a police van in Middleton. The incident is part of a series of similar occurrences in Greater Manchester on the same day.
articles.content_html:
A police officer was rushed to hospital following a crash involving a police van in Middleton. The incident occurred on July 5, 2026, and is one of two similar incidents reported in Greater Manchester on the same day.

Details surrounding the circumstances of the crash have not been disclosed. The officer's condition has not been made public, and further information regarding the incident is awaited.

Local authorities and police are likely to investigate the circumstances leading to the crash, as is standard procedure in such incidents.

articles.title: Edgar Wood Academy Marks First GCSE Completion with Ofsted Praise
articles.excerpt: Edgar Wood Academy celebrates its inaugural Year 11 students completing their GCSEs, following a positive Ofsted report highlighting the school's transformation.
articles.content_html:
Edgar Wood Academy has reached a significant milestone as its first Year 11 students have completed their GCSEs. This achievement comes shortly after the school received a commendable report from Ofsted, which noted the positive changes within the institution since its opening in September 2021.

The academy began with a single Year 7 cohort and has since grown, with students contributing to the establishment of the school's culture and traditions over the past five years. The recent Ofsted inspection praised the calm and purposeful environment fostered by school leaders, highlighting the effectiveness of their strategies in creating a conducive learning atmosphere.

Inspectors reported that students feel safe and supported, with many expressing appreciation for their teachers. The report indicated that students are increasingly proud to be part of the school community, reflecting a positive school spirit.

Headteacher Paul Jones expressed pride in the school's progress and the role of the first cohort in shaping the academy. He emphasised the importance of the supportive environment created for every student, which has been recognised by Ofsted.

The inspection also acknowledged the academy's commitment to student wellbeing, noting that children feel listened to and valued. The report highlighted the school's initiatives to enhance literacy and numeracy, as well as support for students facing learning barriers.

Richard Ronksley, CEO of Altus Education Partnership, remarked on the dedication of staff, families, and students in building the school from its inception. He praised the resilience and ambition demonstrated by the students, marking this achievement as a reflection of their collective efforts.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article was compiled from identified public sources and may be updated when further verified information becomes available.

articles.title: Kirkholt Pantry Celebrates a Decade of Community Support
articles.excerpt: The Kirkholt Pantry has reached its 10th anniversary, celebrating a decade of providing affordable food and support to local families in Kirkholt.
articles.content_html:
The Kirkholt Pantry recently celebrated its 10th anniversary at The Strand, marking a significant milestone in its mission to support local families.

Since its inception, the pantry has distributed over 23,000 bags of essential food items, helping residents cope with the rising cost of living.

The celebration brought together volunteers, customers, and some of the original founders of the pantry, highlighting the community's commitment to this initiative.

Councillor Daniel Meredith, who played a role in establishing the pantry, praised it as a vital resource for residents, providing affordable access to food and alleviating financial pressures.

He expressed gratitude to all volunteers and donors who have contributed to the pantry's success over the years, calling it a testament to community spirit and collaboration.

articles.title: Residents Reflect on Life After Leaving Seven Sisters Tower Blocks in Rochdale
articles.excerpt: As the iconic Seven Sisters tower blocks in Rochdale face an uncertain future, former residents share their experiences of moving out and the changes in their community.
articles.content_html:
Tony Warner, a former resident of the Seven Sisters tower blocks in Rochdale, has shared his thoughts on life after leaving his flat in the iconic buildings. The 60-year-old moved out after receiving a letter from Rochdale Boroughwide Housing (RBH) last October, informing him that he would need to vacate his home of 19 years due to ongoing issues with the flats.

Warner described the area as a 'lifeless stretch of concrete' where the once vibrant community spirit has diminished. He noted that the absence of shops has made daily life more challenging, requiring residents to walk to town for basic necessities.

The Seven Sisters, officially known as College Bank, were once bustling with activity but now have a significant number of empty flats. Warner expressed relief at having moved out, citing high utility bills and cold living conditions as key factors in his decision.

In 2017, RBH announced plans to demolish four of the high-rises, which sparked local opposition. Although demolition was ruled out in 2023, RBH has since reopened discussions about the future of the tower blocks, leaving residents uncertain about their fate.

Campaigners from the 'Save the Seven Sisters' group have raised concerns about the eviction of tenants, suggesting that safety issues may be a cover for redevelopment plans. Despite the uncertainty, Warner encourages others to consider moving out, highlighting the benefits of lower living costs and improved living conditions.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article was compiled from identified public sources and may be updated when further verified information becomes available.
articles.right_to_reply: Anyone directly affected may request a correction or right of reply by emailing news@rochdaledaily.co.uk.

articles.title: Metrolink Lines to Close for Three Weeks Due to Upgrade Works
articles.excerpt: Significant disruptions to tram services are set to occur as part of a £150m upgrade, affecting multiple lines in Greater Manchester.
articles.content_html:
Transport for Greater Manchester (TfGM) has announced that three Metrolink lines will be closed for three weeks starting from 13 July as part of a major £150m upgrade project.

The affected lines include the Eccles, Trafford Centre, and Altrincham routes, with no trams operating during this period. Services on the East Didsbury and Manchester Airport lines will also be impacted, terminating at Firswood instead of their usual destinations.

In addition, the Rochdale line will terminate at Exchange Square during the upgrade. Replacement bus services will be provided to assist passengers between the affected stops.

The upgrade work involves track replacement between Trafford Bar, Cornbrook, and Deansgate-Castlefield, which is known to be one of the busiest tram stretches in the country. TfGM aims to minimise future disruptions by conducting additional work at Eccles and around Piccadilly Gardens during this closure.

A special bus service will operate for The Hundred event at Old Trafford Cricket Ground on 26 and 31 July, ensuring fans can still access the venue despite the tram disruptions.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article was compiled from identified public sources and may be updated when further verified information becomes available.

articles.title: Calls for Reform of Rochdale Rape Legislation by Former Attorneys General
articles.excerpt: Former attorneys general have urged for changes to the laws surrounding rape cases in Rochdale, highlighting the need for legal reform to better protect victims.
articles.content_html:
A group of former attorneys general has called for significant changes to the legal framework governing rape cases in Rochdale. Their statement comes amid ongoing discussions about the adequacy of current laws in addressing the complexities of such cases.

The former officials argue that existing legislation does not sufficiently support victims and fails to reflect the realities of modern society. They believe that reforms are essential to ensure justice is served more effectively.

This push for reform highlights a growing concern within the legal community regarding the treatment of sexual offences and the challenges faced by victims in navigating the legal system.

The call for change has sparked a wider conversation about the need for legislative updates to better align with contemporary understandings of consent and victim support. Advocates are urging lawmakers to take immediate action to address these issues.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article was compiled from identified public sources and may be updated when further verified information becomes available.
